name: Curried Beef Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon minced gingerroot or 1 tsp ground ginger
1 pound boneless sirloin steak, cut into 1/8-inch strips
1 medium green pepper, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 medium sweet red peppers, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine soy sauce, garlic, ginger and 2 tablespoons oil. Add beef; toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for 15-20 minutes.
2. Heat remaining oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Stir-fry beef for 2-3 minutes. Remove beef and set aside.
3. In the same skillet, stir-fry onion for 1 minute; add peppers and celery. Stir-fry for 2 minutes. Return beef to the skillet.
4. Combine water, cornstarch, and curry powder in a bowl until smooth. Add to the skillet. Bring to a boil and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
5. Serve over rice or noodles.
YIELD: 4 servings
